title: Sustainable Budget Act of 2025
stage_label: Referred to the Committee on the Budget, and in addition to the Committee on Rules, for a period to be subsequently dete
plain_summary: This bill creates a special group of 18 people to study the government's money problems and find ways to fix them. The group would have members picked by the President and Congress leaders from both political parties. They need to come up with a plan to balance the government's budget within 10 years and deal with programs like Social Security and Medicare that cost a lot of money. If enough members agree on the plan, the President must ask Congress to vote on it using special rules that make the voting process faster.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on the Budget, and in addition to the Committee on Rules, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall
subjects: Presidents and presidential powers, Vice Presidents

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Sustainable Budget Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Establishment of Commission
sections.text: Within thirty days after this law is passed, Congress will create a special group called the National Commission on Fiscal Responsibility and Reform. This group will have eighteen members. The President will choose six members, but no more than four can be from the same political party. The Senate majority leader will choose three current senators. The House Speaker will choose three current representatives. The Senate minority leader will choose three current senators. The House minority leader will choose three current representatives. All members must be chosen within sixty days after the commission is created. If someone leaves the commission, they will be replaced the same way they were first chosen. The President will pick two members from different political parties to be co-leaders of the commission. All members must have deep experience in government work, money policy, economics, managing government agencies or private companies, public administration, or law. The commission's job is to find ways to improve the government's money situation in the medium term and make it stable for the long term. The commission must suggest ways to balance the budget, not counting interest payments on debt, by the end of ten years from when the commission starts. This will help keep the debt compared to the country's total economic output at an acceptable level. The commission must also suggest ways to improve the long-term money outlook, including changes to address growing entitlement spending and the gap between what the government expects to collect and spend. The commission must vote on a final report with their suggestions within one year after members are chosen. The commission can also vote on temporary reports before the final one. A report can only be released if at least twelve commission members approve it, and at least four members from each major political party support it. Each approved report will be given to Congress and made available to the public. The commission can hold hearings, take testimony, and gather evidence. Federal agencies must give the commission any information it requests. The commission can use the mail system like other government agencies. The commission must create a website that shows their suggestions and records of member attendance at meetings. They must post this information within seventy two hours after each meeting. The Government Accountability Office, Congressional Budget Office, and Joint Committee on Taxation will provide technical help to the commission. Commission members will not receive extra pay but can get travel expenses paid. The co-leaders can hire an executive director and other staff without following normal government hiring rules. The executive director's hiring must be approved by the commission. Staff pay cannot be higher than level five of the Executive Schedule. Government employees can work for the commission temporarily without losing their regular job benefits. The commission can hire temporary workers at rates not higher than the daily equivalent of level five Executive Schedule pay. The commission will end thirty days after it submits its final report. This law does not change the authority of executive departments or agencies or the Office of Management and Budget. It does not create any legal rights that can be enforced in court. Congress can provide money as needed for the commission to do its work. This money stays available until spent. A federal agency means any part of the executive, legislative, or judicial branch of the federal government.

sections.title: Section 3 Consideration of Commission Recommendations
sections.text: Within sixty days after the commission gives its report to Congress, the President must send Congress a special message about the report. This message must include a proposed joint resolution with the legal language needed to put the commission's suggestions into law. Before sending this proposed resolution, the President must talk with Congress. The President must consult with the chairman and top minority member of each Senate and House committee that would be affected by the proposals. These consultations must give committee leaders the chance to suggest other ways to address the commission's ideas and to recommend which suggestions should not be included in the proposed resolution. The President decides which committees are relevant based on what the proposed resolution contains. The President must also work with the head of the Government Accountability Office and the Congressional Budget Office when preparing the proposed resolution. The President's special message must explain which commission suggestions are left out of the proposed resolution and why. It must also explain which suggestions are included and identify programs that the commission says should be eliminated or combined. If the Senate or House is not in session, the President will give the message to the Secretary of the Senate or the Clerk of the House. The President must make the special message and proposed resolution available to the public, including on a presidential website, and publish a notice in the Federal Register explaining how people can get copies.

sections.title: Section 4 Expedited Consideration of Proposed Joint Resolution
sections.text: Only a commission joint resolution gets special fast treatment under this section. A commission joint resolution means a joint resolution that contains only the exact text the President submitted. In the House of Representatives, the majority leader or someone the majority leader chooses can introduce the commission joint resolution the next legislative day after the President submits it. If the majority leader does not introduce it, any House member can introduce it on any legislative day after that. Any House committee that gets the resolution must report it back without changes within ten legislative days. If a committee fails to report it within that time, the House can vote to discharge the committee from considering it. This discharge motion gets twenty minutes of debate split equally between supporters and opponents. If the discharge motion passes, the House immediately considers the resolution. After the last committee reports the resolution or is discharged, the House can move to consider it. The resolution is considered as read, all procedural objections are waived, and it gets two hours of debate split equally plus one motion to limit debate. The House must vote on the resolution within three legislative days after the last committee reports it or is discharged. In the Senate, the majority leader or someone the majority leader chooses can introduce the commission joint resolution the next legislative day after the President submits it. If not introduced that way, any senator can introduce it on any day the Senate is in session after that. The resolution goes to the appropriate committees, which must report it with a recommendation within ten session days. If committees fail to report it, they are automatically discharged and the resolution goes on the calendar. Within two session days after all committees report or are discharged, the majority leader or designee can move to consider the resolution. Any senator can make this motion after the two day period ends. The motion to proceed is not debatable and cannot be delayed. All procedural objections are waived. The resolution cannot be amended, postponed, sent to other business, or sent back to committee. Appeals about Senate rules for this resolution are decided without debate. Commission joint resolutions cannot be amended in either chamber. If one chamber receives a commission joint resolution from the other chamber before passing its own, the received resolution is not sent to committee and the same procedures apply until the final vote, when the received resolution replaces the chamber's own version. This does not apply if the House receives a tax measure from the Senate. If one chamber fails to consider a commission joint resolution, the other chamber's resolution gets the same fast procedures. If the President vetoes a commission joint resolution, Senate consideration of the veto message gets ten hours of debate split equally between majority and minority leaders. These rules are part of each chamber's rules but only apply to commission joint resolutions. Each chamber can change these rules at any time using normal procedures.
